config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru membatasi kekuasaan agar tidak semena-mena. Dalam praktik, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, dan saya melihat itu berbahaya. Bagi saya, yang tepat adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of rule is risky because it can easily become a license for governments or majorities to trample minorities in the name of the "greater good." Society does matter, and rights are not absolute in every situation, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit on individual freedom, not the other way around. A healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first and then making limited, necessary compromises when there is a real public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga yang lemah. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu tidak diprioritaskan sebagai batas, negara dan mayoritas bisa seenaknya mengatasnamakan ketertiban atau moralitas. Saya juga tidak setuju jika hak individu dianggap penghalang; justru hak individu yang sehat membuat kepentingan bersama tidak berubah jadi alat penindasan. Jadi, menurut saya yang benar bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan perlindungan hak yang kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse by governments and majorities. The strongest counterpoint is that society does sometimes need limits on individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and subject to real checks so they do not become a blank check for power. In my view, rights are not obstacles to the common good; they are the guardrails that keep “the common good” from turning into mob rule or state overreach. If a policy truly serves society, it should be able to withstand scrutiny without first crushing the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dijadikan alasan untuk menekan warga yang lemah atau berbeda. Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama supaya negara dan mayoritas tidak mudah memakai dalih ketertiban, moralitas, atau keamanan untuk bertindak semena-mena. Memang ada keadaan tertentu ketika kepentingan umum perlu dibatasi sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan prinsip bahwa masyarakat selalu lebih penting dari individu. Kalau hak individu dilemahkan duluan, yang sering terjadi bukan kebaikan bersama, melainkan kekuasaan yang makin leluasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that principle is dangerous because it gives governments and majorities too much room to define "the common good" however they want, which often ends up hurting minorities or unpopular individuals. I do think society can justify limited restrictions in real emergencies, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and heavily checked. In my view, individual rights are not a barrier to a healthy society; they are what keep society from becoming coercive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
